candidates.quote: but Edmure had thrown his reserve at them, and they had shattered and reeled away bloody and beaten.
candidates.note: riverlands/Riverrun side, defended the Red Fork crossings against Tywin

candidates.quote: Lord Tywin had tried to force a crossing at a dozen different fords, her brother wrote, but every thrust had been thrown back.
candidates.note: westerlands/Lannister side, tried to force the Red Fork

candidates.quote: but the fiercest battle had been fought at Stone Mill, where Ser Gregor Clegane had led the assault.
candidates.note: Lannister side, led the Stone Mill ford assault (sub-command under Tywin); repelled by Edmure's reserve

candidates.quote: We were all horsed,” Ser Brynden said. “The Lannister host was mainly foot. We planned to run Lord Tywin a merry chase up and down the coast, then slip behind him to take up a strong defensive position athwart the gold road, at a place my scouts had found where the ground would have been greatly in our favor.
candidates.note: strategic advisor to Robb/Edmure; his alternate plan (scouted Goldroad position) was the one NOT followed, which he criticizes Edmure for

candidates.quote: We did not cross half the world to wait. Our best chance is to strike hard and fast, before King’s Landing knows who we are. I mean to take Storm’s End.
candidates.note: Golden Company/Aegon side, originates and orders the attack

candidates.quote: I want the attack to go ahead … with one change. I mean to lead it.
candidates.note: Golden Company/Aegon side; insists on personally leading the assault over Connington's plan

candidates.quote: Homeless Harry Strickland disagreed. “We should wait.”
candidates.note: Golden Company captain-general; advisory/planning role in the war council deciding to attack, not a combat leader for this specific assault (Connington/Aegon lead it)

candidates.quote: Lord Stannis and a small garrison had held the castle for close to a year, against the great host of the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne.
candidates.note: Baratheon/rebel side, defender/castellan

candidates.quote: Cloaked in that darkness, Davos the smuggler had dared the Redwyne cordon and the rocks of Shipbreaker Bay alike.
candidates.note: logistical/supply role, ran the blockade with food that kept the garrison alive; not a combatant

candidates.quote: “I came down on Storm’s End to lift the siege,” Ned told them, “and the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne dipped their banners, and all their knights bent the knee to pledge us fealty.
candidates.note: Baratheon/rebel side, led the relief force that broke the siege

candidates.quote: who had once held Storm’s End through a year of siege, surviving on rats and boot leather while the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne sat outside with their hosts, banqueting in sight of his walls.
candidates.note: besieging side (Reach host); surname reference "Lord Tyrell" was Mace Tyrell at this date, corroborated by node Origins text

candidates.quote: who had once held Storm’s End through a year of siege, surviving on rats and boot leather while the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne sat outside with their hosts, banqueting in sight of his walls.
candidates.note: besieging side, commanded the Redwyne fleet that closed Shipbreaker Bay; surname reference corroborated by node Origins text

candidates.quote: He’d only taken the black after he’d lost an arm at the siege of Storm’s End.
candidates.note: wounded (arm lost to festering axe wound) during the siege, defending side

candidates.quote: before I could reach a decision Lord Balon Greyjoy rose in rebellion against the Usurper, and Ned Stark called his banners to help his friend Robert.
candidates.note: rebel/ironborn side, self-proclaimed king

candidates.quote: when the stag and the direwolf had joined to end the pretensions of the self-proclaimed King of the Iron Islands.
candidates.note: crown side; "the stag" = Robert

candidates.quote: before I could reach a decision Lord Balon Greyjoy rose in rebellion against the Usurper, and Ned Stark called his banners to help his friend Robert.
candidates.note: crown side, allied commander who called his own banners to aid Robert

candidates.quote: Prince Jaehaerys and his sisters, Rhaena and Alysanne, descended upon the castle on their dragons.
candidates.note: arrives at King's Landing on dragonback ahead of Rogar's host, takes the Red Keep's holdouts prisoner

candidates.quote: A fortnight later, Lord Rogar Baratheon and Queen Alyssa arrived at King’s Landing with their host, and hundreds more were seized and imprisoned.
candidates.note: leads the host that follows up Jaehaerys's dragon-arrival and completes the takeover

candidates.quote: We will take them under cover of this darkness.
candidates.note: Targaryen side, orders the night attack

candidates.quote: “Your Grace, I bring you victory. The Stormcrows turned their cloaks, the slaves broke, and the Second Sons were too drunk to fight, just as you said.
candidates.note: Targaryen side, commander of Dany's army per node Origins

candidates.quote: “If battle is joined, let Grey Worm show wisdom as well as valor,” Dany told him.
candidates.note: Targaryen side, Unsullied captain leading the flank assault

candidates.quote: Daario upended the sack, and the heads of Sallor the Bald and Prendahl na Ghezn spilled out upon her carpets.
candidates.note: killed the other Stormcrow captains and turned the company to Dany's side before the battle began

candidates.quote: Daario upended the sack, and the heads of Sallor the Bald and Prendahl na Ghezn spilled out upon her carpets.
candidates.note: Stormcrow captain, killed by Daario in the run-up to the battle

candidates.quote: Daario upended the sack, and the heads of Sallor the Bald and Prendahl na Ghezn spilled out upon her carpets.
candidates.note: Stormcrow captain, killed by Daario in the run-up to the battle

candidates.quote: Ser Criston sat on his white horse shouting, “Aim for the rider,” through the smoke and flame.
candidates.note: greens side, sprang the trap he had planned

candidates.quote: The crimson jaws of Meleys closed round Sunfyre’s golden neck for a moment, till Vhagar fell upon them from above.
candidates.note: greens side; Vhagar's dive delivered the fatal blow to Rhaenys/Meleys

candidates.quote: A body believed to be Rhaenys Targaryen was later found beside the carcass of her dragon, but it was so blackened that no one could be sure it was her.
candidates.note: blacks side, killed with her dragon Meleys

candidates.quote: Two more winged shapes appeared: the king astride Sunfyre the Golden, and his brother Aemond upon Vhagar.
candidates.note: greens side, rode Sunfyre into the dragon battle and was badly burned

candidates.quote: Another hundred perished not long after, when Prince Aemond and Ser Criston Cole took Rook's Rest and put its garrison to death.
candidates.note: castellan of Rook's Rest, killed with his garrison after the dragon battle

candidates.quote: I took this castle and I mean to hold it, to live or die as Prince of Winterfell.
candidates.note: ironborn/defending side

candidates.quote: I have near two thousand men with me . . . and if the tales be true, you have no more than fifty.
candidates.note: Stark side, castellan leading the relief force

candidates.quote: When the old fool gave me his hand, I took half his arm instead. Then I let him see my face.
candidates.note: killed during the parley by Ramsay (Red Helm); distinct from his COMMANDS_IN role earlier in the same battle

candidates.quote: When the old fool gave me his hand, I took half his arm instead. Then I let him see my face.
candidates.note: Ramsay's own confession of killing Rodrik Cassel during the parley, disguised as "Reek"/Red Helm

candidates.quote: Ser Rodrik seemed to have the numbers, but the Dreadfort men were better led, and had taken the others unawares.
candidates.note: led the Dreadfort relief force (as "Red Helm") that broke the northern siege of Winterfell; distinct combat-command role from the AGENT_IN killing of Rodrik

candidates.quote: With Leobald Tallhart and Cley Cerwyn.” The boy lord had taken an arrow in the eye
candidates.note: young Lord Cerwyn, killed in the battle

candidates.quote: With Leobald Tallhart and Cley Cerwyn.
candidates.note: commanded a Tallhart contingent in the relief force, among the dead corpses brought to the gates

candidates.quote: Sixteen years ago, a bastard son of King Aegon IV named Daemon Blackfyre had risen in revolt against his trueborn brother.
candidates.note: rebel/Blackfyre side

candidates.quote: His revolt had ended on the Redgrass Field, where Daemon and his twin sons died beneath a rain of Lord Bloodraven's arrows.
candidates.note: loyalist side, commanded the archers that broke the rebellion at Redgrass Field

candidates.quote: Sixteen years ago, a bastard son of King Aegon IV named Daemon Blackfyre had risen in revolt against his trueborn brother.
candidates.note: loyalist/crown side; Daeron II is the "trueborn brother" whose throne was contested

candidates.quote: His head was smashed in by a mace wielded by a lord with three castles on his shield." Lord Gormon Peake.
candidates.note: rebel/Blackfyre side, killed Roger of Pennytree at Redgrass Field with his mace

candidates.quote: Shawney was with Bittersteel on the right, and almost died of his wounds.
candidates.note: rebel/Blackfyre side ("Bittersteel"), commanded the right flank of Daemon's host at the Battle of the Redgrass Field; escaped into exile afterward

candidates.quote: Caggo was the one who finally cut him down, fighting through the king’s protectors on his monstrous warhorse and opening Cleon the Great from shoulder to hip with one blow of his curved Valyrian arakh.
candidates.note: Windblown/Yunkai-allied side, killed Cleon in the sortie battle beneath the walls

candidates.quote: Caggo was the one who finally cut him down, fighting through the king’s protectors on his monstrous warhorse and opening Cleon the Great from shoulder to hip with one blow of his curved Valyrian arakh.
candidates.note: already-dead body propped up to lead the Astapori sortie; cut down again by Caggo

candidates.quote: Frog had done his part in the slaughter that followed, riding down the frightened eunuchs with the other Windblown.
candidates.note: disguised as "Frog," squire to Greenguts, fighting in the Windblown company

candidates.quote: The three Dornishmen spilled from the tent together to join the fighters sprinting for the horse lines.
candidates.note: disguised as "Dornish Gerrold," fighting in the Windblown company

candidates.quote: The three Dornishmen spilled from the tent together to join the fighters sprinting for the horse lines.
candidates.note: disguised as "Greenguts," fighting in the Windblown company

candidates.quote: I built a fleet at Robert’s command, took Dragonstone in his name.
candidates.note: led the fleet and conquest of Dragonstone

candidates.quote: I built a fleet at Robert’s command, took Dragonstone in his name.
candidates.note: orderer/instigator; commanded Stannis to build the fleet and take the island but did not personally execute it

candidates.quote: one night Ser Willem Darry and four loyal men had broken into the nursery and stolen them both, along with her wet nurse, and set sail under cover of darkness for the safety of the Braavosian coast.
candidates.note: smuggled Viserys and infant Daenerys off Dragonstone before Stannis's fleet arrived, denying him his prize

candidates.quote: In the end, the second Daemon Blackfyre rode forth alone, reined up before the royal host, and challenged Lord Bloodraven to single combat.
candidates.note: rebel/Blackfyre side, styled himself Daemon II Blackfyre, King

candidates.quote: Instead, Lord Bloodraven's men surrounded him, pulled him off his horse, and clasped him into golden fetters.
candidates.note: loyalist side, Hand of the King, commanded the royal host that surrounded Whitewalls and captured Daemon

candidates.quote: Lord Peake himself was storming across the muddy field to climb the steps to the herald's platform, his cloak flapping behind him. "We are betrayed!" he cried.
candidates.note: rebel side, chief instigator/orchestrator of the Whitewalls conspiracy; node already carries an in:SUSPECTED_OF edge from this event, this COMMANDS_IN is a distinct confirmed role, not a duplicate

candidates.quote: "I told you." Dunk stabbed him through the throat. "I'm better with a sword."
candidates.note: killed Ser Tommard Heddle in single combat when Heddle tried to seize Prince Aegon ("Egg") for the conspirators

candidates.quote: "I told you." Dunk stabbed him through the throat. "I'm better with a sword."
candidates.note: conspirator, commanded Lord Butterwell's garrison; harm-gate note: this node is event.war (VICTIM_IN not permitted here) even though the text shows Heddle killed in single combat by Duncan the Tall -- routed to FIGHTS_IN per SHARED-RULES harm-gate guidance rather than forcing VICTIM_IN onto the war umbrella

candidates.quote: That was where Robert had made his breach, swarming in over the rubble and corpses with his warhammer in hand and Ned Stark at his side.
candidates.note: crown side, personally led the breach assault

candidates.quote: That was where Robert had made his breach, swarming in over the rubble and corpses with his warhammer in hand and Ned Stark at his side.
candidates.note: crown side, fought at Robert's side in the breach

candidates.quote: Theon had watched from the safety of the Sea Tower, and sometimes he still saw the torches in his dreams, and heard the dull thunder of the collapse.
candidates.note: child at the time, watched the battle (including his brother Maron's death) from the Sea Tower; the perception itself is load-bearing (recurring nightmare)

candidates.quote: Rodrik had been slain by Lord Jason Mallister at Seagard, Maron crushed in the collapse of the old south tower
candidates.note: killed when the south tower collapsed during Robert's breach

candidates.quote: The final battle was on Pyke. When Robert’s stonethrowers opened a breach in King Balon’s wall, a priest from Myr was the first man through, but I was not far behind. For that I won my knighthood.
candidates.note: crown side, won his knighthood for valor in the breach

candidates.quote: When the walls of Pyke crashed down, he was the first through the breach. He fought with one of his flaming swords, setting ironmen afire with every slash.
candidates.note: crown side, first man through the breach, wielding a wildfire-coated sword

candidates.quote: where Robert had accepted the rebel lord’s surrender and Ned had taken his son Theon as hostage and ward
candidates.note: ironborn/defending side, surrendered his fallen stronghold to Robert

no_node.name: Prince Aegon (son of Aenys I, "Aegon the Uncrowned")
no_node.note: rebelled against Maegor prior to the events of this uprising node (his own rebellion/death is a preceding event, not part of the Jaehaerys uprising itself); did not search for a node since he is out of scope for this event's role edges

flags: battle-at-pyke: three Stauntons/etc.-style ambiguity does not apply here, but note Ramsay Snow (R2-33/R2-34) carries two distinct roles (AGENT_IN killing Rodrik Cassel during a parley, and COMMANDS_IN leading the Dreadfort relief force) on battle-outside-the-gates-of-winterfell -- both are textually distinct beats in the same scene, kept as two edges per the 'rare but text-supported' allowance.

battle-outside-the-gates-of-winterfell: the wiki Aftermath section itself flags an in-universe dispute over whether Theon or Ramsay struck the blow that killed Rodrik Cassel (Ramsay's own boast in acok-theon-06 vs. later Roose Bolton's account to Robb in ASOS crediting Ramsay differently). R2-32/R2-33 follow the on-page ACOK scene (Ramsay's own words to Theon), which is the most direct evidence available; flagging the discrepancy per SHARED-RULES proven-vs-suspected guidance.

siege-of-storms-end: Mace Tyrell and Paxter Redwyne (R2-11/R2-12) are grounded via the surname-only book phrase 'the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne' rather than full given names; cross-checked against the node's own Origins section (which names Mace Tyrell and Paxter Redwyne specifically as the besieging lords at this date) to resolve the reference. No plausible alternate Lord Tyrell/Redwyne existed at the time of Robert's Rebellion, so confidence is tier-1, but noting the referential indirection for reviewer awareness.

first-blackfyre-rebellion and second-blackfyre-rebellion: Fire & Blood (fab/*.md) in this corpus stops short of Daeron II/Aegon IV's reigns (confirmed via fab-lineages-and-family-tree-25.md, an author-interview transcript stating Blackfyre Rebellion material is reserved for a future F&B volume). All grounding for these two war nodes therefore comes from tmk-dunk-01.md (The Mystery Knight) backstory/flashback references rather than a direct F&B narrative chapter -- still book-text (tier-1), just noting the source shift from the expected fab/ directory.

taking-of-storms-end vs siege-of-storms-end: kept strictly separate per prompt guidance -- taking-of-storms-end (R2-05/06/07) draws only from adwd-the-griffin-reborn-01 (Golden Company/Jon Connington/Aegon plot); siege-of-storms-end (R2-08 through R2-13) draws only from Robert's-Rebellion-era material (acok-prologue, agot-eddard-06/10, agot-jon-03). No participants were cross-attached.

taking-of-storms-end: Arianne Martell / Haldon / Daemon Sand content in the packet's Narrative Arc and Quotes sections cites 'Arianne II'-style chapters that do not exist in this corpus (they are unreleased material, not part of the five published books under sources/chapters/). Could not ground any Arianne-side role edges in book text; omitted rather than forced into wiki_only, since the packet's own Quotes block already carries that content as wiki-sourced prose.

second-blackfyre-rebellion: R2-53 (Tommard Heddle) initially drafted as VICTIM_IN but the node is event.war, which fails the VICTIM_IN harm-gate per SHARED-RULES -- corrected to FIGHTS_IN. The underlying single-combat killing (Duncan the Tall vs. Tommard Heddle at Whitewalls) is a genuine harm-subtype beat that currently has no dedicated event node of its own; flagging in case a future pass wants to mint a sub-event for it the way battle-of-the-redgrass-field exists for the First Blackfyre Rebellion.
